define: verb. To determine with precision; to mark out with distinctness; to ascertain or exhibit clearly.
deft: adjective. Quick and neat in action; skillful.
den: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Slavey. (macrolanguage)
dene: noun. A valley, especially the deep valley of a stream or rivulet.
dent: noun. A shallow deformation in the surface of an object, produced by an impact.
dint: noun. Especially in by dint of: force, power.
dit: verb. To stop up; block (an opening); close.
dite: verb. To prepare for use or action; to make ready.
edit: noun. A change to the text of a document.
eft: noun. A newt, especially a smooth newt (Lissotriton vulgaris, syn.
endite: noun. An additional lobe on the inner side of the protopodite of a crustacean limb.
fed: noun. A federal government officer or official.
fee: noun. An amount charged for a privilege.
feed: verb. To give (someone or something) food to eat.
fete: noun. A festival open to the public, the proceeds from which are often given to charity.
feted: verb. simple past and past participle of fete
fid: noun. A pointed tool without any sharp edges, used in weaving or knotwork to tighten and form up weaves or complex knots; used in sailing
fie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fyer.
fiend: noun. A devil or demon; a malignant or diabolical being; an evil spirit.
fin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Finnish.
find: verb. To locate To encounter or discover by accident; to happen upon.
fine: adjective. Senses referring to subjective quality.
fined: verb. simple past and past participle of fine
finite: adjective. Having an end or limit; constrained by bounds; whose number of elements is a natural number.
fit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Meänkieli.
indie: adjective. Independent; from outside the mainstream.
indite: verb. To physically make (letters and words) on a writing surface; to inscribe.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
need: noun. A requirement for something; something needed.
neif: noun. A woman born in the state of serfdom; a female serf.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
ted: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tepo Krumen.
tee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Huehuetla Tepehua.
teen: noun. Synonym of teenager: a person between 13 and 19 years old (inclusive).
teiid: adjective. Of or belonging to the Teiidae family of tropical American lizards.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tend: verb. To make a tender of; to offer or tender.
tide: noun. The daily fluctuation in the level of the sea caused by the gravitational influence of the moon and the sun.
